output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 6/6.
Batch 1: Batch 1 / T1 completed. Shared pure resize math now lives in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts`, both `TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `useClipResize.ts` call it, focused unit coverage was added in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_1.json`. Verified with the full affected test modules.
Batch 2: Batch 2 / T2 completed. `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now captures `cursorOffsetPx` for `clip`, `group-child-boundary`, and `group-edge` sessions from the actual rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down, and live preview math now flows through `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` instead of `initialClientX` deltas. Off-center pointer coverage was added to `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Batch 3 / T3 completed. `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now uses a single ref-backed resize preview store exposed through `useSyncExternalStore`, render and pointer/commit paths read the same snapshot, and `resizeOverrides` has been removed as a second mutable authority. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Batch 4 / T4 completed. Added a shared timeline scale conversion layer with `createTimelineScale` and `useTimelineScale`, then rewired the touched pixel/time conversion sites in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimeRuler.tsx`, `drop-position.ts`, and `useClipDrag.ts` to use that single contract. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Batch 5 / T5 completed. `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` now includes a stateful harness with pointer-level assertions for an off-center interior-boundary drag and a delegated outer-left group-edge drag, both checking exact live boundary tracking during drag and no boundary jump on release. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Batch 6 / T6 completed. The temporary pinned-group interior-boundary repro script was created, passed, and deleted. The targeted resize validation passed (`3` files / `31` tests), the full video-editor suite passed (`42` files / `222` tests), and `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still fails only on pre-existing non-video-editor paths; a follow-up grep found no `src/tools/video-editor/` type errors. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/clean-up-the-structural-20260408-1813/execution_batch_6.json`.
commands_run: rg -n "keeps the interior pinned-group boundary fixed on release|keeps the group outer-left boundary fixed on release|anchors interior pinned-group boundary preview" src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx
rg -n "first-edge \(left edge of first clip in group\)|last-edge \(right edge of last clip in group\)" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx
npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts $(rg --files src/tools/video-editor | rg '\.test\.(ts|tsx)$')
deviations: Used `npx vitest` for verification because `vitest` was not available on PATH in this environment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Created `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts` with pure `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize`, removed duplicated grouped boundary clamp/min-duration logic from `TimelineCanvas.tsx` preview and `useClipResize.ts` commit paths, preserved group-edge child projection locally in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, and added direct unit coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`.

Updated `TimelineCanvas.tsx` so every resize session kind captures `cursorOffsetPx` from the rendered boundary pixel at pointer-down and derives live preview positions from `cursorBoundaryPixel -> time` instead of `initialClientX` delta math. Preserved existing snap behavior, audio preview clamps, and `onActionResizing` behavior. Added off-center pointer coverage in `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`.

Replaced the dual preview authority in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` with one ref-backed resize preview store exposed through `useSyncExternalStore`, updated render paths and pointer/commit paths to read that single snapshot, and removed `resizeOverrides` as a separate mutable authority from the component. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, plus grep confirmation that `resizeOverrides` no longer appears in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` while `useSyncExternalStore` and the new preview store do.

Introduced `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-scale.ts` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineScale.ts`, then rewired the touched conversion sites in `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimeRuler.tsx`, `drop-position.ts`, and `useClipDrag.ts` to use the shared conversion contract instead of local `scaleWidth / scale` pixel-time math. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-scale.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, plus grep confirmation that the touched conversion sites now use `useTimelineScale` or `createTimelineScale` and no longer use ad hoc `scaleWidth / scale` math.

Extended `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` with a stateful harness around the real `TimelineCanvas` pointer flow, then added pointer-level assertions for an off-center interior boundary drag and an outer-left group-edge drag that both verify exact live boundary tracking during move and no boundary jump on release. Kept `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx` unchanged and verified its existing first-edge/last-edge group-edge commit semantics still pass. Verified with the full affected modules: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx`, plus grep confirmation of the new pointer tests and the existing first/last-only commit tests.
commands_run: rg -n "keeps the interior pinned-group boundary fixed on release|keeps the group outer-left boundary fixed on release|anchors interior pinned-group boundary preview" src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx
rg -n "first-edge \(left edge of first clip in group\)|last-edge \(right edge of last clip in group\)" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.test.tsx

task_updates.executor_notes: Ran the mandatory throwaway pinned-group interior-boundary repro as a temporary vitest file under `src/tools/video-editor/`, confirmed it passed, and deleted both temporary repro files afterward. Then ran the required targeted resize modules and the full video-editor suite. The targeted run passed (`3` files / `31` tests), and the full video-editor suite passed (`42` files / `222` tests). `npx tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still fails, but the reported errors are in non-video-editor areas such as `src/domains/*` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/*`; a follow-up grep over the typecheck output found no `src/tools/video-editor/` errors, so there are no new video-editor type regressions from this cleanup.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts $(rg --files src/tools/video-editor | rg '\.test\.(ts|tsx)$')

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed by direct grep and module-level tests: `computeGroupChildBoundaryResize` and `computeGroupEdgeResize` exist as pure helpers in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.ts`, have direct unit coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resize-math.test.ts`, and are called from both `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts`.

Confirmed by code inspection and module-level verification: all three resize session kinds now store `cursorOffsetPx` from the real boundary pixel at pointer-down, `TimelineCanvas.updateResize()` computes `cursorBoundaryPixel` and `cursorBoundaryTime`, and no live preview path still uses `initialClientX` delta math.

Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and module-level verification: `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now has one authoritative ref-backed resize preview store exposed through `useSyncExternalStore`, renderers and pointer handlers read the same snapshot, and `resizeOverrides` has been removed as a second mutable authority.

Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and module-level verification: the touched timeline conversion sites now use the shared scale abstraction (`useTimelineScale`) or pure conversion factory (`createTimelineScale`) instead of ad hoc `scaleWidth / scale` math.

Confirmed by code inspection, grep, and full-module verification: `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` now contains pointer-level tests that assert exact live interior-boundary and group-edge boundary tracking during drag and no boundary jump on release, while `useClipResize.test.tsx` still locks in the existing first/last-clip-only group-edge commit semantics.

Confirmed by direct execution logs: the temporary pinned-group interior-boundary repro script was created, run successfully, and deleted; the required targeted resize modules and full video-editor suite were rerun afterward and passed; `tsc -p tsconfig.app.json --noEmit` still has baseline failures outside `src/tools/video-editor`, and a follow-up grep found no `src/tools/video-editor/` type errors.
